document.docAuthor: Zhang, Bing-Lan, Yan, Cheng-Jin, Achterberg, Cornelis van, Peng, Yan-Qiong & Chen, Hua-Yan
document.docTitle: Lycogaster umbonata Chen & van Achterberg 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Integrated taxonomy unveils new species of Trigonalyidae (Insecta, Hymenoptera) from Yunnan, China

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Integrated taxonomy unveils new species of Trigonalyidae (Insecta, Hymenoptera) from Yunnan, China

mods:name.mods:affiliation: School of Life Sciences, Sun Yat-sen University, Guangzhou 510275, China
Wenzhou Vocational School of Science and Technology, Wenzhou 325300, China & Wencheng Modern Agriculture and Health Industry Research Institute, Wenzhou 325300, China

State Key Laboratory of Rice Biology and Ministry of Agriculture, Hangzhou 310058, China & Key Lab of Agricultural Entomology, Institute of Insect Sciences, Zhejiang University, Hangzhou 310058, China
CAS Key Laboratory of Tropical Forest Ecology, Xishuangbanna Tropical Botanical Garden, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Mengla 666303, China
Key Laboratory of Plant Resources Conservation and Sustainable Utilization, South China Botanical Garden, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Guangzhou 510650, China

figureCitation.captionText: Figure 4. Lycogaster umbonata Chen & van Achterberg, sp. nov., holotype, female (En- 419021) A habitus, lateral aspect B head, anterior aspect C head, dorsal aspect.

Figure 5. Lycogaster umbonata Chen & van Achterberg, sp. nov., holotype, female (En- 419021) A wings B mesosoma, dorsal aspect C mesosoma, lateral aspect D metasoma, dorsal aspect E metasoma, lateral aspect F metasoma, ventral aspect.

Figure 6. Lycogaster umbonata Chen & van Achterberg, sp. nov., paratype, male (En- 419022) A habitus, lateral aspect B head, anterior aspect C metasoma, dorsal aspect D metasoma, lateral aspect E metasoma, ventral aspect.

subSubSection.paragraph: Antenna elongate and hardly widened medially (Figs 4A , 6A ); second sternite of female with a strong lobe-shaped protuberance medio-apically (Fig. 5E ); mandibles with ivory patches (Figs 4B , 6B ); second tergite of female strongly shiny and with elliptical depressions antero-laterally (Fig. 5D ); wing membrane subhyaline and without darkened (Figs 4A , 5A , 6A ).

caption.paragraph:
Figure 6.
Lycogaster umbonata
Chen & van Achterberg, sp. nov., paratype, male (En-419022) A habitus, lateral aspect B head, anterior aspect C metasoma, dorsal aspect D metasoma, lateral aspect E metasoma, ventral aspect.

subSubSection.paragraph: Female. Holotype, length of body 5.1 mm (of fore wing 4.1 mm).

Wings .
Fore wing: length of vein 1-M 1.8 x as long as vein 1-SR; second submarginal cell 1.4 x as long as third submarginal cell (Fig. 5A ).
Named after the strong lobe-shaped protuberance of female second sternite: from " umbo " (Latin for "rounded protuberance").

Holotype
, female, China : Yunnan , Baoshan City , Longyang Dist. ,
2390 m
, 25°17'57.26"N , 98°46'11.35"E , 15-30.IX.2020 , Lang Yi leg., MT, SYSBM En-419021 (deposited in SYSBM); GenBank : OM057968
.
Paratype
, 1 male , China : Yunnan , Baoshan City , Longyang Dist. ,
2390 m
, 25°17'57.26"N , 98°46'11.35"E , 30.VII-15.VIII.2020 , Lang Yi leg.,MT, SYSBM En-419022 (SYSBM); GenBank : OM057967
.

The key to Chinese species of
Lycogaster
published by Chen et al. (2014) could be updated to accommodate
L. umbonata
by replacing couplet 2 as follows:

tr.td: Female antenna elongate and hardly widened medially (Fig. 4A ); second tergite smooth and strongly shiny (Fig. 5D ); medial third of fore wing subhyaline (Figs 4A , 5A , 6A ) or slightly darkened
Female antenna less elongate and distinctly widened medially; second tergite punctate and moderately shiny; medial third of fore wing largely dark brown
Medio-apical protuberance of second sternite with pair of small submedial acute teeth close to each other; posterior end of middle lobe of mesoscutum rugose

td.emphasis:
L. angustula
Chen, van Achterberg, He & Xu

L. umbonata
Chen & van Achterberg, sp. nov.
